* fix: wrap local HTTP server lifecycle in try/finally for reliable cleanup in requestTimestamp tests

Co-authored-by: Avi-Bendetsky <146357256+Avi-Bendetsky@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Avi-Bendetsky <146357256+Avi-Bendetsky@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(watchdog): 15s delay before startup AC resume — prevent double-resume race

server.py startup hook and platform_watchdog both fire resume_stuck_ac_cycles()
concurrently at startup, creating duplicate asyncio tasks for the same session.
15s delay gives the startup hook time to register sessions in _active_mission_tasks
before the watchdog checks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add `touying-raw` for animated code block reveals (#283)

* Initial plan

* Initial plan: implement touying-raw feature

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add touying-raw for animated code blocks with pause/meanwhile

- Fix touying-mitex bug: utils.label -> utils.label-it
- Add touying-raw function with block, lang, fill-empty-lines, simple params
- Add _parse-touying-raw helper for line-by-line and simple-mode parsing
- Handle touying-raw kind in _parse-content-into-results-and-repetitions
- Export touying-raw from src/exports.typ
- Add test at tests/features/raw/test.typ

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* update

* fix: simple mode inline #pause;/#meanwhile; no longer causes line breaks

In simple mode, the previous implementation split each text segment by \n,
added lines to result-lines, then joined with \n. When markers appeared
inline (e.g. "fn main() { #pause;println!(...); }"), this introduced
spurious newlines where none existed in the original source.

Fix: work directly with text strings. Visible segments are concatenated
as-is (preserving original newlines). Hidden segments with fill-empty-lines
use .replace(regex("[^\n]+"), "") to keep line count but erase content.

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* tests

* fmt

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <318483724@qq.com>

commit.message:
fix: refresh button updates milestone + phase status without restart

Root cause: /api/sprints/:n/state returned 'init' for sprints without
a state file, even when the milestone was closed on GitHub. The phase
badge in the header showed 'INIT' instead of 'COMPLETE'.

Fixes:
- State endpoint checks knownMilestones for closed milestones and
  returns phase='complete' when milestone is closed
- Refresh flow is sequential: first updates milestone list (so backend
  has fresh data), then fetches state + issues
- Single ↻ click now updates: issues, milestone status, phase badge

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Detect switch statements with >10 branches (giant_switch)
- Detect type switches with >10 branches (giant_type_switch)
- Detect if-else chains with >10 conditions (giant_if_else_chain)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: make LSP code actions configurable via setup() (#4)

Move the hardcoded LSP code action definitions out of
lsp-code-actions.lua and into config.lua as a default
lsp_code_actions list, matching the pattern used for keymaps.

Add Aiwaku.LspCodeAction type to types.lua with a required
title and optional prompt prefix field. Extend Aiwaku.Config
with the new lsp_code_actions field.

Refactor lsp-code-actions.lua to read from
state.config.lsp_code_actions at generator call time instead
of maintaining a hardcoded list. Guards against missing/empty
titles with a WARN notification and skips those entries.
Add null-ls load guard that returns {} with a WARN when
null-ls is not available.

Users can now override or extend the default action list
during setup():

  require("aiwaku").setup({
    lsp_code_actions = {
      { title = "Send to Aiwaku" },
      { title = "AI: write tests", prompt = "write tests for:" },
    },
  })

Update README.md and doc/aiwaku.nvim.txt to document the
new lsp_code_actions option and how to override the defaults.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: re-encrypt SOPS files and prevent future MAC corruption (#370)

- Re-encrypt app-nfs.sops.yaml and app-iscsi.sops.yaml to fix MAC
  mismatch caused by Renovate modifying targetRevision without
  re-running sops (commit bae10e0)
- Add Renovate packageRule for SOPS files: disable automerge, label
  with sops-reencrypt so PRs still notify but require manual
  re-encryption before merge
- Add GitHub Action to block PRs that modify SOPS files without
  properly re-encrypting (checks lastmodified timestamp)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(shared): add Zod runtime validation for all API responses (E-10)

Create response-schemas.ts with 40+ Zod schemas covering every API
response shape consumed by both user-dashboard and admin apps.

Infrastructure:
- New @indexnow/shared/response-schemas entry point (tsup)
- validateApiResponse() utility: logs warnings, never throws
- api<T>() in user-dashboard accepts optional schema parameter
- Admin hooks call validateApiResponse() after JSON parsing

Coverage (35 validated call sites):
- user-dashboard: 17 query/mutation hooks + 3 billing mutations
- admin: 15 query hooks across all 13 hook files
- Void/unknown mutations excluded (nothing to validate)

All schemas use .passthrough() for forward compatibility —
extra fields are allowed, missing required fields are caught.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Keep the recent parse/render perf cleanups in the same validated changeset.

commit.message:
docs: Restructure documentation architecture and sidebar hierarchy (#1678)

- [x] Explore repository structure and current docs layout
- [x] Create About section (move introduction.md → about/_index.md for
en and ja)
- [x] Add "About" link to top navigation in hugo.toml (weight=10)
- [x] Create usage/ subdirectory with _index.md (from usage.md) for en
and ja
- [x] Move supported-formats.md, image-resizing.md, quality-control.md
into usage/ for en and ja
- [x] Update frontmatter weights: Installation=10, Usage=20,
Deployment=30, Production=40, FAQ=90
- [x] Update weights within usage/: Supported Formats=10, Image
Resizing=20, Quality Control=30
- [x] Fix about/_index.md layout by adding `type: docs` to frontmatter
(en + ja)
- [x] Add "Docs" link (weight=20) to main navigation menu in hugo.toml

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Docs: Restructure documentation architecture and sidebar
hierarchy</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> The current documentation sidebar is flat and poorly ordered, making
it difficult for users to navigate. Features related to "Usage"
(Supported Formats, Image Resizing, Quality Control) are scattered,
"Production Guide" and "Deployment Guide" are not adjacent, and the
"FAQ" is not at the bottom. Additionally, the "Introduction" (What is
Manael) clutters the technical docs and should be moved to a dedicated
`/about/` section.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Restructure the Hugo (Docsy) content directories and adjust the
`weight` frontmatter across all files to create a logical, hierarchical
flow.
> 
> **Target Structure:**

commit.message:
fix: last 3 case-sensitive state comparisons — root cause of stale issues

The initial issue load in registerWeb() and the milestone state check
in ws-server.ts both compared i.state === 'closed' (lowercase) against
gh CLI's 'CLOSED' (uppercase). This was the ACTUAL root cause — the
active sprint's initial issue list was built with the wrong comparison,
and since it's the active sprint, handleSprintIssues always served
this stale data via getIssues() callback.

Fixed all 3 remaining locations. Total: 6 locations across 3 files
had this bug — now all use .toLowerCase().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix required checks detection: add rulesets fallback and pending checks guard (#146)

* Initial plan

* Fix required checks detection: add rulesets fallback and pending checks guard

- has_required_checks_for_pr() now falls back to the repository rulesets
  API (GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rules/branches/{branch}) when the legacy
  branch protection endpoint returns 404, detecting required checks
  configured via GitHub's newer rulesets system.
- New has_pending_checks_for_pr() method checks for queued/in-progress
  check runs on the PR head commit as a safety net.
- Both run_needs_verification() and advance_tracked_prs() now check for
  pending checks before merging directly, preventing premature merges
  when checks haven't completed.
- Same fixes applied to worker/src/github.rs for consistency.

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: issue cache always fetches from GitHub — removes stale saved-state path

Root cause: loadSprint() tried saved state first, mapping all issues
to 'planned' regardless of actual GitHub state. Even after closing
an issue on GitHub, the cache would show it as 'planned' because the
saved state was created at planning time and never updated.

Fix: loadSprint() now always fetches from GitHub via fetchFromGitHub().
Saved state was unreliable for issue status — it only knew about the
planning snapshot, not runtime state changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(platform): slot-switch stability — aclose crash + AC resume

- bus.py: catch RuntimeError('aclose()') on Redis async generator shutdown
  (Python 3.12 race on SIGTERM) — prevents blue slot crash loop
- platform_watchdog.py: resume_stuck_ac_cycles() now uses DB-driven
  valid_ids instead of hardcoded _AC_PROJECTS list — works for all projects
  including new ones (ac-docsign-clone, ac-ecommerce-solaris, etc.)

VM-side (already applied):
- slot-watchdog.sh: guard before failover — if AC sessions running,
  restart active slot instead of switching (preserves running cycles)
- macaron-platform-blue/green.service: ExecStop marks active sessions
  as interrupted before stop, enabling clean resume on new slot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix naming convention: rename ReporterType to Type

Eliminates package stuttering in internal/reporter by renaming
ReporterType to Type, following Go naming conventions. The type
is only used within the reporter package, so the shorter name
improves readability without losing clarity.

Changes:
- Renamed type ReporterType to Type in generator.go
- Updated all references in simple_reporter_test.go
- Updated audit findings in AUDIT.md

Tests: All tests pass (go test -race ./...)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- ui/Button: variants, sizes, loading, disabled, icons, click handler
- ui/StatusBadge: colors, variants, sizes, rounding, icons
- dashboard/AdopterNudge: render conditions, dismiss, add org action
- dashboard/DiscoverCardsPlaceholder: carousel, add/browse actions, filtering

- dashboard/SmartCardSuggestions: delay, suggestions, add, dismiss
